topic.what: Fq-linear subcodes of lambda-Gabidulin codes for public-key encryption
topic.how: Generator-matrix construction of random subcodes avoiding stabilizer invariants, applied to MinRank-based Niederreiter encryption
topic.why_matters: Achieves smallest ciphertext sizes among compared rank-metric schemes while maintaining competitive public-key sizes for post-quantum security

characteristics.vs_baseline: Smallest among EGMC-Niederreiter, Classic McEliece, BIKE, RQC variants, MinRankPKE
Competitive with EGMC-Niederreiter and BESG; larger than BIKE/RQC

characteristics.what_itasures: Likelihood of random subcode having trivial matrix stabilizer

characteristics.evidence: Work factor Cf from kernel attack, support-minors, hybrid approaches

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fq-dimension ratio of subcode to parent code

not_for: Low-field characteristic (q=2) where stabilizer failures more common
requires: Random subcode filtering for stabilizer avoidance

novelty: Relation between lambda-Gabidulin and classical Gabidulin subspace subcodes via coordinate scaling
Generator-matrix construction for random Fq-linear subcodes avoiding algebraic invariants
LGS-Niederreiter cryptosystem with smallest ciphertext sizes among rank-metric schemes
Stabilizer/annihilator algebra analysis for subspace subcode distinguishers
open_problems: Sharper structural criteria for random Fq-linear subcodes behaving like random matrix codes
Complexity of recovering hidden parent expanded code from public subcode
Extension to other structured rank-metric primitives beyond Gabidulin
Practical security bounds against improved MinRank solvers
